text: Hi, welcome to This is My Architecture. I am Priya and today we have with us Nishant Rathod from BookMyShow.

Welcome Nishant. Hello Priya. Can you tell us a little bit about BookMyShow? Sure Priya. BookMyShow is India's leading entertainment destination with global operations and the one-stop shop for every entertainment needs.
The firm has its presence in more than 650 towns and cities in India and with the support from our partners, we provide the best entertainment to our users for millions of customers.
So typically how many users do you have?
So typically, you know, post the pandemic we are getting around 250 million users and 5 billion page views per month on average.
So that's quite a lot of volume. So are you going to explain to us how the data architecture is handling that volumes?

Sure Priya. So we have all this data available in different sources in BookMyShow and these are the kind of data like transaction data, the CIM data, even the clickstream data and the data from various products.
So all this data available in different sources. So all this data available in different sources and we use EMR to fetch data from the sources.
So EMR clusters are running to fetch data from different sources and all these data are then stored in our data link which is S3.
Once the data is available in S3, we use them for cataloging which is done using Glue and we again use the EMR clusters which are transient in nature to process the data and store it in Redshift.
So all the data are now available in Redshift which is our data warehouse and can be used for NIRDs and reporting purposes.
That's interesting. So where are the business users actually visualizing this data which you have in a data warehouse?

So once we have the data in Redshift, we use QuickSight which is a business interdistance tool available in AWS to create various dashboards which can be used for business, products and marketing team to report and analyze the data.
Okay. And are you using SageMaker for machine learning?
Yeah. So SageMaker we have our own in-house data science team which uses SageMaker to process the data for users personalization and audience segmentation.

We have various machine learning models running on SageMaker which gets data from S3 which is the source for the SageMaker and this data I use to create personalized experience users for our millions of customers.
And also for various audience segmenting which can be used for promoting content on BookMyShow.
I hear that BMS sold 28 million tickets in the month of April. Can you tell us how this architecture helped you scale?
Yes. So during the month of April 22, we had the highest sales ever recorded in BookMyShow.
BookMyShow had re-engineered the whole data pipeline from the scratch while migrating to AWS.
So this helped us scale all the services and maintain the cost during this high month.
And another interesting thing that I would like to point here is that, you know, during the highest sales which is ever recorded in BookMyShow,
the data platform scaled to its peak but still the cost was 70% less than the on-frame data architecture.
That's interesting. You saved 70% of the cost. Can you elaborate how you actually achieved that?
Yeah. So we achieved that using the serverless services that we have in AWS which is S3 and Glue.
And apart from that, the EMR clusters which can auto scale as per the traffic.
So this helps us, you know, in maintaining the cost. And also we use transient EMR clusters for processing the batch data.
And in those transient clusters, we use spot instances in the instance fleet which can, you know, reduce the cost of EMRs.
That's interesting. Can you also tell us how long it took for you to migrate to this new architecture?
Yes, Pia. So the whole migration from the on-frame system was done during the pandemic in four months of duration.
And we use, you know, various services like, you know, step functions which can, you know, orchestrate the EMRs jobs.
And also using Infra as a code service available in AWS which is CloudFormation template,
we were able to deploy the whole system in a short amount of time.
It's interesting that in such a short time you could build such a scalable architecture.
Thank you, Nishant, for walking through this data architecture.
And thank you all for watching This Is My Architecture.
